title: AI Accountability Coach For Side Projects

input.idea: AI accountability coach for people trying to finish side projects.
input.target_user: Individuals trying to finish a personal side project while juggling work, school, or life.
input.use_case: They would use it when momentum drops and they need accountability to keep shipping small progress.
input.user_context: User requested a B2C Persona run.

evidence_packet.claim: Focusmate sells virtual body doubling with scheduled sessions, partners, and millions of completed focus sessions.
evidence_packet.relevance: The behavior people pay for is external presence and scheduled commitment, not advice alone.

evidence_packet.claim: stickK uses commitment contracts, money stakes, referees, supporters, and goal communities.
evidence_packet.relevance: Accountability demand exists, but credible pressure often comes from stakes or humans.

evidence_packet.claim: Coach.me combines habit tracking with human coaching, community encouragement, and reviews.
evidence_packet.relevance: An AI coach competes with trackers, community, and human coaches.

evidence_packet.claim: A habit-coach chatbot study found procedural interaction design mattered more than static advice.
evidence_packet.relevance: The hard part is behavior design, not motivational text.

verdict_metrics.name: likely pull
verdict_metrics.reason: Side-project guilt is common, but many users churn from coaching apps

verdict_metrics.name: urgency
verdict_metrics.reason: The pain is recurring, but rarely urgent enough to pay immediately

verdict_metrics.name: willingness to pay
verdict_metrics.reason: Payment needs visible progress, stakes, or community

verdict_metrics.name: trust barrier
verdict_metrics.reason: Users may like encouragement but doubt an AI can create accountability

verdict_metrics.name: differentiation
verdict_metrics.reason: Habit trackers, body doubling, and human coaching already cover the job

hard_nos: If the product sends motivational check-ins, users will ignore it like any other notification.
If there is no external stake, it is weaker than Focusmate, stickK, or a friend.
If it asks users to plan too much, procrastinators will churn before the first win.
If it treats all side projects alike, advice will feel generic and low-trust.
objections_to_beat: Prove users complete one visible project milestone within 7 days, not just open the app.
Add one accountability mechanism users cannot easily dismiss: public commitment, money stake, or booked session.
Narrow to one project type first, such as shipping a landing page or publishing a demo video.
Test whether users prefer AI nudges, human check-ins, or scheduled coworking before building a coach.

next_experiment.action: Run a 7-day SMS concierge with 15 side-project builders; each picks one deliverable, posts a public commitment, and receives daily yes/no progress checks plus one unblock prompt.
next_experiment.why: This tests whether lightweight accountability changes behavior before building an AI coach.
next_experiment.success_signal: At least 6 publish the deliverable, 8 reply on 5 or more days, and 3 offer to pay $10 for another week.

personas.label: Daily blocked builder
personas.objection: They want help but have trained themselves to ignore reminders.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A daily check-in tied to one tiny deliverable and visible streak loss.

personas.label: Casual optimizer
personas.objection: They will try it once and move on.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A free challenge that creates peer pressure quickly.

personas.label: Paid productivity buyer
personas.objection: They already buy tools, but expect a clear mechanism.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A 7-day outcome guarantee around one shipped artifact.

personas.label: Privacy-sensitive skeptic
personas.objection: They will not share unfinished personal work with an AI.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: Local-only mode or human-free metadata tracking.

personas.label: Aspirational creator
personas.objection: They like the identity more than the work.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A public micro-launch challenge that makes progress socially visible.

personas.label: Budget-constrained student
personas.objection: They need accountability but will not pay much.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A cohort or campus challenge with free entry and optional upgrade.

personas.label: Habit-tool loyalist
personas.objection: They already have Todoist, Notion, streaks, or Focusmate.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: An integration that uses their current task list and adds stakes.

personas.label: Impulse trier
personas.objection: They create noisy signups and vanish before habit formation.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A one-hour challenge with immediate visible output.

personas.label: High-stakes finisher
personas.objection: They need to finish for a deadline, portfolio, or application.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: Deadline mode with human backup and proof of completion.

personas.label: Careful comparer
personas.objection: They will compare against coaching, Focusmate, and habit trackers.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: Published completion rates from a focused cohort.

personas.label: Community-led builder
personas.objection: They respond to peer momentum more than private AI chat.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A cohort challenge where the AI organizes accountability between people.

personas.label: Workaround user
personas.objection: They prefer notes, calendars, or occasional guilt.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A pain spike they cannot solve with existing routines.
